questions.prompt: Complete: "My two sisters ___ nurses at the local hospital."
questions.explanation: My two sisters refers to more than one person, so it takes are: My two sisters are nurses. Is is for a single he/she/it subject, am is only for I, and be is the base form — not a present-tense form on its own.

questions.prompt: Complete: "Leila is tired. ___ is in bed."
questions.explanation: Leila is a girl's name, so the correct pronoun to replace it is she: She is in bed. He replaces a man's name, they replaces a group of people or things, and we includes the speaker — none of those fits Leila alone.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct negative contraction: "The classroom ___ very clean today."
questions.explanation: The classroom is singular (it), so the negative is isn't: The classroom isn't very clean today. Aren't is for you/we/they, don't belongs to ordinary verbs (not to be), and "not is" puts not before the verb — the wrong order.

options.text: There are five students in the room.

questions.explanation: The error is (c): I takes am, not are — the correct form is I am very happy. Sentences (a), (b) and (d) all use the right form of to be: is with a singular subject, are with two people joined by and, and are with the plural five students.

questions.prompt: Your answer is YES. Choose the correct short answer to "Are your parents at home?"
questions.explanation: A positive short answer with are is not contracted, so say Yes, they are (not Yes, they're). "Them" is an object pronoun, never the subject of a short answer; and "are they" is question order, not an answer.

questions.prompt: Complete: "The quality of these shoes ___ really good."
questions.explanation: The real subject is the quality (singular), not shoes — the phrase "of these shoes" is just a modifier. So the verb is is: The quality of these shoes is really good. This is the same trap as "The price of books is…" — find the head noun before picking the verb form.
